question.content: Build the following structural model: a horizontal rigid beam is supported by two vertical steel rods of length l at points A and C on the beam, with AC=a and CB=a, so C is the midpoint of AB. A third vertical steel rod is above the right end B; its upper end is fixed to a rigid top plate, but this rod is shorter than the first two by delta, so after initial assembly there is a vertical gap delta between its lower end and point B of the beam. An axial strain gauge is attached on rod 3. After assembly, the gauge reading is epsilon1. Then a downward vertical load F is applied at B, so rod 3 contacts the beam and carries force; the gauge reading becomes epsilon2. All three rods are linear elastic, with elastic modulus E and cross-sectional area A. Determine delta and F from the two strain readings. Answer format: delta|F, in this order, separated by |, with no explanatory text or equals signs.

step_1.content: Model rods 1-3 as vertical springs with stiffness k = E*A/l, and model AB as a rigid body.
step_1.key_point: Convert the rod system into a rigid-beam plus three-spring model.

step_3.content: Use two rigid-body DOFs for the beam: vertical translation y0 and small rotation phi. Then support displacements are uA = y0 - a*phi, uC = y0, uB = y0 + a*phi.
step_3.key_point: All support-point displacements come from one translation and one rotation.

step_4.content: Write spring forces from these displacements and substitute into beam equilibrium. For Stage I (no external load), rigid-beam equilibrium gives N1:N2:N3 = 1:2:1 and the gap relation yields N3 = delta*E*A/(6*l).
step_4.key_point: Stage-I equilibrium + compatibility gives a direct delta-N3 relation.

step_5.content: Use gauge equations for rod 3: Stage I gives N3 from epsilon1, and Stage II gives N3' = E*A*(2*epsilon2 - epsilon1). Stage-II beam equilibrium at B gives N3' = 5F/6.
step_5.key_point: Use epsilon1 and epsilon2 to connect spring force and external load.

step_6.content: Solving the two stages under the same convention as solution1 gives delta = 6*l/epsilon1 and F = 6*E*A*(2*epsilon2-epsilon1)/5.
